Title: Glucosinolate hydrolysis products suppress entomopathogenic nematodes in vitro but do not protect sequestering flea beetle larvae in vivo

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against natural enemies; toxic to entomopathogenic nematodes and their symbiotic bacteria in vitro
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of glucosinolate hydrolysis products in P. armoraciae larvae, but reduction of its activity does not increase larval susceptibility to Heterorhabditis bacteriophora in vivo
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi silencing of PaMyr2 reduced larval myrosinase activity by 78% to 96%. 2. PaMyr2 silencing reduced 2-propenyl isothiocyanate by 82% and 3-butenenitrile by 76% in larvae. 3. Despite lowered hydrolysis-product levels, PaMyr2 silencing did not significantly alter larval mortality or red cadaver coloration after H. bacteriophora infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dsRNA-mediated RNAi silencing; myrosinase activity assay; GC-MS/GC-FID metabolite quantification; EPN infection survival assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in larvae based on functional RNAi experiments
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S amplicon sequencing of bacterial community (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to Heterorhabditis bacteriophora; feeding on Brassica juncea or Arabidopsis genotypes; dsRNA injection
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights insect myrosinase-mediated activation of sequestered glucosinolates as a host-defense trait, but this trait alone is insufficient to confer protection against EPN infection in vivo
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing lowers hydrolysis-product levels without improving survival against EPNs; defense may instead shape post-infection microbial interactions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is the principal experimentally manipulated insect defense gene in the Results. Although enzymatic, it is not a plant nutrient-biosynthetic pathway gene but a defense activation gene, so classified here as Common Gene.
